findings.description: Broken internal reference to a nonexistent section
findings.evidence: "Security considerations are documented in Section 6." The document contains five sections: Overview, Architecture Components, Performance Characteristics, Operational Notes, Conclusions. There is no Section 6.
findings.suggestion: Either add a Security section to the document or replace this sentence with the actual location of security documentation (e.g., an external document link).

findings.description: Undefined retry policy referenced by name but never specified
findings.evidence: "Transformation applies the standard retry policy on transient failures." No retry policy is defined anywhere in this document — retry count, backoff strategy, and failure escalation are all absent.
findings.suggestion: Define the retry policy inline (e.g., exponential backoff, max 3 attempts) or reference a specific external document where it is defined.

findings.description: Overflow queue path for large files is mentioned but not elaborated for a final architecture brief
findings.evidence: "Large files are handled separately from the primary transformation path via an overflow queue." No further detail is provided: no size threshold, no processing difference, no rejoining the main path.
findings.suggestion: Specify the size threshold that triggers overflow routing, how overflow processing differs, and where overflow results rejoin the pipeline.

findings.description: Authentication mechanism mentioned without any specifics
findings.evidence: "Authentication is used to verify each request before processing begins." No mechanism is named (API key, OAuth, mTLS, etc.).
findings.suggestion: Name the authentication mechanism or reference the security specification where it is documented.

findings.description: Component referenced only in a performance claim but never introduced as an architectural element
findings.evidence: "The in-memory delivery cache reduces end-to-end latency by 99.9% compared to unbuffered writes." This cache is not described in any architecture component section and appears nowhere else in the document.
findings.suggestion: Introduce the delivery cache as a named component within Stage 3, describing its role and configuration, before making performance claims about it.

out_of_lens_observations.note: Overview states "All pipeline stages run in parallel to maximize throughput" but Stage 2 states "Stage 2 must complete before Stage 3 begins." This is a direct contradiction. Belongs to Coherence lens.
